task.title: Align CodeWiki loop skills with compiler lexicon
task.summary: Rename primary loop skills so the packaged skill surface matches the canonical feedback, documentation, and implementation loops, while keeping validation/research/architecture/audit as support skills.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki packaged skills guide the three canonical loops with names and descriptions that match the lexicon: feedback, documentation, and implementation.
acceptance: Primary loop skills are named `codewiki-feedback`, `codewiki-documentation`, and `codewiki-implementation`.
The `codewiki` router skill routes to the three loop skills and labels validation/research/architecture/view-audit as gateway/support skills.
Skill frontmatter descriptions use loop terms as activation triggers and avoid generic `plan`/`task` vocabulary for primary loops.
README, package smoke tests, KB code_paths, and related skill references are updated.
Old the old documentation-loop skill name and the old implementation-loop skill name skill directories and references are removed, not aliased or deprecated.
non_goals: Do not change loop semantics or build schemas.
Do not make skills the source of truth.
Do not keep backward-compatible aliases for old loop skill names.
verification: Run grep for stale the old documentation-loop skill name and the old implementation-loop skill name references in live docs/source/tests/roadmap.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm graph maps the new skill paths.

summary: TASK-051 implemented: primary loop skills now match the compiler lexicon exactly with no deprecated aliases. The package exposes codewiki-feedback, codewiki-documentation, and codewiki-implementation as the three loop skills, with support skills kept separate.
checks_run: grep live docs/source/tests/roadmap for old primary loop skill names

evidence: Smoke test expected package skills now includes codewiki-feedback, codewiki-documentation, and codewiki-implementation with no old loop skill names.
Live grep confirms old primary loop skill names are absent from README, skills, KB, source, smoke tests, package.json, roadmap, and current TASK-051 builds.
notes: Tester verified package skill discovery and stale-name absence.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Renamed skill directories and frontmatter to codewiki-documentation and codewiki-implementation.
Updated router skill, feedback handoff, README, KB code_paths, package smoke expectations, and session status key.
Removed old skill directories instead of adding aliases.
notes: Builder applied direct rename and fixed tests/source references without compatibility aliases.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Smoke test expected package skills now includes codewiki-feedback, codewiki-documentation, and codewiki-implementation with no old loop skill names.
Live grep confirms old primary loop skill names are absent from README, skills, KB, source, smoke tests, package.json, roadmap, and current TASK-051 builds.
code_change_evidence: Renamed skill directories and frontmatter to codewiki-documentation and codewiki-implementation.
Updated router skill, feedback handoff, README, KB code_paths, package smoke expectations, and session status key.
Removed old skill directories instead of adding aliases.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Primary loop skills are named codewiki-feedback, codewiki-documentation, and codewiki-implementation.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: skills directory and frontmatter contain the three loop skill names; smoke test expects those exact package skills.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: The codewiki router skill routes to the three loop skills and labels validation/research/architecture/view-audit as support skills.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: skills/codewiki/SKILL.md lists feedback/documentation/implementation as loop skills and verify/research/architecture/view-audit separately.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Skill frontmatter descriptions use loop terms as activation triggers and avoid generic plan/task vocabulary for primary loops.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: codewiki-documentation and codewiki-implementation frontmatter names/descriptions use canonical documentation and implementation compiler terminology.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: README, package smoke tests, KB code_paths, and related skill references are updated.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: README skill list/layout, scripts/smoke-test.mjs expected skills, .codewiki/kb/system/compilers.md, and product automation code_paths point at new skill paths.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Old primary loop skill directories and references are removed, not aliased or deprecated.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: old documentation and implementation loop skill directories do not exist; live grep for old names is clean; no alias skill directories were added.

risks: Pi must reload to see renamed skill package resources.

commit.title: refactor(skills): align loop skill names with compiler lexicon
commit.body: Rename primary loop skills to codewiki-feedback, codewiki-documentation, and codewiki-implementation with no aliases. Update router, README, smoke tests, KB code paths, and session status naming. Checks: tsc, architecture, npm test, stale-name grep, codewiki_state refresh.

pr.title: refactor(skills): align loop skill names with compiler lexicon

checks_recorded: grep live docs/source/tests/roadmap for old primary loop skill names

validation_verdict.rationale: Loop skill rename aligns with accepted feedback, documentation, source, tests, and graph.
